_schema._note: Navigator-owned, git-tracked, internal-only. NOT published to wiki or user-facing surfaces. Accumulates signal on wiki `file_bug` filers so filer-reputation can feed the (deferred) fair-distribution calculator when bounty pool activates. Update after every vet pass. Schema is flexible — solidifies once we have volume. Do not delete records; historical signal matters.

<filer_identity>.identity_note: How we know this filer — 'chatbot (UNIVERSE_SERVER_USER=anonymous)', 'github_handle:alice', 'chatbot paste-pattern shared with X/Y/Z (probably same session)', etc. Identity resolution is best-effort until file_bug captures explicit filer_id field.
<filer_identity>.first_seen: ISO-8601 date of first vetted filing
<filer_identity>.last_seen: ISO-8601 date of most recent vetted filing
<filer_identity>.reshape_depth_note: 0 = no rewrite, 1 = tighter scope only, 2 = partial reshape (approach changed), 3 = full reshape (only the problem statement survives). Average across reshaped filings.
<filer_identity>.quality_score_formula_note: Provisional: (shipped * 1.0 + reshaped * 0.5 - rejected * 0.5) / max(filings_total, 1). Refine when volume + real payouts land.

notable_filings.title: short descriptive — NOT the BUG-NNN id per gate rule
notable_filings.navigator_note: freeform observation — injection-attempt flag, unusually-well-scoped, repeat topic, etc.

chatbot_common_paste_pattern.identity_note: Wiki bug bodies sharing a consistent stray-XML-tag residue (<parameter name=...> / </observed>). Seen across all 18 pages of the 2026-04-22 batch. Not confirmed same filer; pattern suggests a chatbot paste-leakage that's session-level, not malicious. Treat as best-effort single-entity until file_bug captures explicit filer_id.

notable_filings.title: char-split on string input_keys
notable_filings.navigator_note: Already-landed at vet time. Clean narrow bug report; filer identified exact repro + preferred-fix shape correctly.

notable_filings.title: multi output_keys silently drops past first
notable_filings.navigator_note: Bundle with sibling typed-output no-op — same root cause. Filer named both halves clearly. Real run_id cited for repro. Narrowed to Option A per dev audit.

notable_filings.title: typed non-str output_keys not written to state
input_keys isolation not enforced for prompt_template
notable_filings.navigator_note: Filer named user-sim isolation as the load-bearing use case; well-scoped. Dev already had a working-tree diff matching my Pass-1 recommendation.

notable_filings.title: list_branches node_count double-counts
notable_filings.navigator_note: Trivial one-line arithmetic bug. Filer identified the exact mismatch with describe_branch.

notable_filings.title: maintainer_notes field for builder prose
notable_filings.navigator_note: Original spec rejected as substrate duplication + injection-superhighway. Reshaped to describe_branch.related_wiki_pages (option B). Filer's underlying need was real; proposed implementation was wrong. Credit-split question when bounty pool lands: filer identified the discoverability gap; navigator built a different answer.

notable_filings.title: brace-escape in prompt_template
notable_filings.navigator_note: Filer's proposed fix (make {{x}} literal) would silently break every Claude.ai-authored prompt_template. Reshaped to backslash-escape + build-time validation + docstring fix. Filer hit a real pain point but misdiagnosed the fix; underlying validation-timing complaint was correct.

notable_filings.title: conditional_edges not exposed on build/patch
notable_filings.navigator_note: Filer correctly diagnosed half-built feature (field exists on model, missing on MCP surface). Also named the use case (iteration loops / pass-fail gates) explicitly.

notable_filings.title: empty LLM response cascade
notable_filings.navigator_note: Already-landed at vet time (41079b6 + 810ae83). Filer correctly correlated with daemon-dormant bug.

notable_filings.title: run_branch durability across daemon restart
notable_filings.navigator_note: Split into two specs: v1-contract-documentation (shipped) + SqliteSaver-keyed resume (was strategy-open, host answered 2026-04-22, now dev-dispatchable). Filer asked both halves correctly — I originally collapsed into one; lead's two-halves correction surfaced this.
